attention1.ptxt: Be sure to keep transaxle with torque converter horizontal or facing up throughout work. Should it be tilted with torque converter down, converter may fall off and cause personal injury.

s1.ptxt: Dismount transaxle with engine. Refer to Engine Assembly Removal and Installation .
Lock drive plate by engaging flat-bladed screwdriver or the like (1) with drive plate ring gear.
Remove drive plate to torque converter bolts through installation hole of starting motor.
Remove bolts and nuts fastening engine and transaxle, then detach transaxle from engine.

attention3.ptxt: When detaching transaxle from engine, move it in parallel with crankshaft and use care so as not to apply excessive force to drive plate and torque converter.

s1.ptxt: Make sure that torque converter is installed correctly to transaxle.
Tighten bolts and nuts to specified torque.
Confirm that there is one different bolt (No. 1 bolt) in drive plate to torque converter bolts.

item.ptxt: Drive plate to torque converter No. 1 bolt (1): Positioning bolt with taper
Drive plate to torque converter No. 2 bolt (2): Standard bolt

s1.ptxt: Lock drive plate by engaging flat-bladed screwdriver or the like (1) with drive plate gear.
Align a bolt hole of drive plate with that of torque converter.
Engage the drive plate and torque converter by tightening the drive plate to torque converter No. 1 bolt through installation hole of starting motor.

torqueitem.t-part: Drive plate to torque converter No. 1 bolt

s1.ptxt: Tighten drive plate to torque converter No.2 bolts in the same manner.

torqueitem.t-part: Drive plate to torque converter No. 2 bolt

s1.ptxt: Remount engine with transaxle assembly to vehicle.
Refer to Engine Assembly Removal and Installation for its procedure.
